sections.description: Implementation plans directory containing 13 sequential implementation phases plus an index file. Each plan is a markdown document that outlines the objectives, design decisions, and acceptance criteria for a specific phase of the lil-olt-metrics project development.
sections.purpose: Serve as a comprehensive roadmap and reference for the project implementation, documenting each phase from foundation through completion. These plans guide development, track progress, and provide historical context for architectural decisions.
sections.functionality: Contains 13 completed implementation plans (01-13) that cover the full development lifecycle: project foundation setup, configuration system, core data types, in-memory storage, OTLP-to-Prometheus translation, OTLP metrics ingestion, query API, server wiring, filesystem persistence, remote backend support, extended OTLP features, integration tests, and documentation. Each plan includes problem statements, design approaches, and acceptance criteria. A .context.md index file provides navigation and overview of all plans.
sections.files: 01-project-foundation.md (project setup, go.mod, directory structure, build tools), 02-configuration.md (env var config, default values, validation), 03-core-types.md (metric types, labels, aggregation), 04-memory-store.md (in-memory data structure, aggregation logic), 05-translator.md (OTLP to Prometheus translation), 06-otlp-ingestion.md (HTTP ingestion endpoint, protobuf parsing), 07-query-api.md (metrics query endpoints, label queries), 08-server-wiring.md (router setup, dependency injection), 09-fs-persistence.md (metrics file storage and recovery), 10-remote-backends.md (remote Prometheus/Grafana integration), 11-extended-otlp.md (additional OTLP features), 12-integration-tests.md (end-to-end test suite), 13-documentation.md (API docs, user guides), .context.md (index and navigation)
sections.strengths: Comprehensive sequential roadmap covering all major project components; clear separation of concerns with one phase per feature; detailed design documentation including acceptance criteria; serves as both development guide and architectural reference; supports onboarding and historical understanding of design choices
sections.weaknesses: Static markdown documents that may drift from implementation; no automated verification that plans match actual code; plans are linear and assume sequential execution; doesn't track deviations from original plans or post-hoc changes
sections.test_coverage: No test coverage. This is documentation/planning material, not executable code. Quality assurance is through human review of plans before implementation begins.
sections.dependencies: No code dependencies. Plans are written in Markdown and reference the project structure but don't import or depend on any code modules. They serve as input to implementation phases which then produce actual code.
